sourceMovie.title: The First Great Train Robbery
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The First Great Train Robbery (1978) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Night of the Hunter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The First Great Train Robbery clicked for you, The Night of the Hunter (1955) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The First Great Train Robbery?
faq.answer: Try The Night of the Hunter, Flawless, Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows—each shares DNA with The First Great Train Robbery while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The First Great Train Robbery?
faq.answer: The First Great Train Robbery (1978) is best described as Thriller, Adventure, Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The First Great Train Robbery worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
